sections.title: Summarize and molt deliberately
sections.body: Use progressive disclosure for tool results. Raw output is for inspection; only summarize a result after you have consumed/digested it. When you are continuing in the same session, once a completed tool result is consumed and its raw text no longer needs inspection, strongly prefer summarizing it regardless of length to lower active context. This is not only an overflow tactic: it reduces token per API call and improves cache/continuation efficiency. Keep raw output visible only when you still need to inspect it, quote it, or compare details. Batch already-digested results into one summarize call when possible rather than discharging each immediately. Better still, avoid ingesting bulky raw output into main context at all: bulky/mechanical/repetitive work (full test suites, large log scans, large diffs, issue sweeps, batch edits/validation) should usually be delegated to a daemon/claude-p or an appropriate daemon body — frame the task, give exact paths/commands and expected artifacts, then review the daemon's concise report instead of pulling noisy output back just to summarize it later. Use daemons to keep raw bulk out of context; use summarize for the bulk that already landed there. Two cadences help. Urgent: when a tool result is long/noisy or raises a large-result reminder, read it, keep conclusion/key evidence/paths or IDs/validation/risks/next steps, then summarize that completed prior result on a later step once it no longer needs full inspection. Idle cleanup: when work quiets down, check `_meta.agent_meta.current_tool_result_chars.total_chars` and `.top_results`; for listed tool results, consider summarize if deemed useless, already digested, irrelevant, or obsolete so active context keeps grain rather than raw bulk. The original remains recoverable from logs by tool_call_id (for example grep events.jsonl or use lingtai-agent log query); the summary should preserve enough IDs and evidence to make that recovery possible. If an adapter/provider comment is present in `_meta.agent_meta.adapter_comment` or the resident meta_guidance section, follow its additional adapter-specific summarize rules (for example provider cache/epoch economics or thresholds) on top of these general rules. Summarize is tool-result hygiene; molt is the stronger summarize boundary and the main way to lower overall conversation context and improve token efficiency. If you have already decided to molt, do not pay a separate summarize call merely to prepare for that molt; tend durable stores/session journal and molt instead. When the current task is complete, necessary reporting/durable stores are tended, and there is no concrete next action, molt deliberately regardless of the current context size. The reason is token economy and cache health: carrying a finished task segment into future turns raises token per API call and can reduce cache/continuation efficiency. If summarize/reconstruction cannot bring context below 0.7 of the window, tend durable stores and molt deliberately.
